title: Write-Through Cache Write Operation
description: Algorithm for handling write operation in Write-Through cache policy
code:
PROCEDURE WriteThrough(address, data)
 // Find cache line for the address
 cacheLine <- FindCacheLine(address)
 
 IF cacheLine IS NOT NULL THEN
 // Write hit: update cache
 cacheLine.data <- data
 cacheLine.valid <- TRUE
 END IF
 
 // Always write to main memory
 MEMORY[address] <- data
 
 // Wait for memory write to complete
 WAIT_FOR_MEMORY_WRITE()
 
 RETURN WRITE_COMPLETE
END PROCEDURE
explanation: In Write-Through, every write updates both cache and main memory. The operation completes only after memory write finishes, ensuring consistency but with higher latency.

title: Write-Back Cache Write Operation
description: Algorithm for handling write operation in Write-Back cache policy
code:
PROCEDURE WriteBack(address, data)
 cacheLine <- FindCacheLine(address)
 
 IF cacheLine IS NOT NULL THEN
 // Write hit: update cache only
 cacheLine.data <- data
 cacheLine.valid <- TRUE
 cacheLine.dirty <- TRUE // Mark as modified
 RETURN WRITE_COMPLETE
 ELSE
 // Write miss: handle based on allocation policy
 // Using write-allocate here
 evictedLine <- FindEvictableLine()
 
 // Write back if dirty
 IF evictedLine.dirty THEN
 MEMORY[evictedLine.address] <- evictedLine.data
 END IF
 
 // Load new block from memory
 newBlock <- MEMORY[address to address+blockSize]
 cacheLine.data <- newBlock
 cacheLine.address <- address
 cacheLine.valid <- TRUE
 cacheLine.dirty <- TRUE
 
 // Update with new data
 cacheLine.data[offset] <- data
 
 RETURN WRITE_COMPLETE
 END IF
END PROCEDURE
explanation: Write-Back updates only cache on write, sets dirty bit. Memory is updated only when the cache line is evicted. Uses write-allocate on miss.

complexity.time: O(1) cache hit, O(block) miss with write-back

title: Cache Line Eviction in Write-Back
description: Algorithm for handling cache line eviction with dirty bit management
code:
PROCEDURE EvictCacheLine(cacheLine)
 IF cacheLine.dirty THEN
 // Write modified data back to memory
 startAddr <- cacheLine.address
 blockSize <- CACHE_BLOCK_SIZE
 
 FOR i FROM 0 TO blockSize - 1 DO
 MEMORY[startAddr + i] <- cacheLine.data[i]
 END FOR
 
 // Clear dirty bit after write-back
 cacheLine.dirty <- FALSE
 END IF
 
 // Mark line as invalid (ready for new data)
 cacheLine.valid <- FALSE
 
 RETURN EVICTION_COMPLETE
END PROCEDURE
explanation: Before evicting a cache line in Write-Back, the dirty bit is checked. If set, the modified data must be written back to main memory before the line is replaced.

complexity.time: O(blockSize) if dirty, O(1) if clean
